denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 01/30/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100055433          Group #: GRP-1007
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-104980
Claim #: CLM-2026-188372
Date of Service (requested): 01/10/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/12/2026 for A1 pulley release related to Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: A1 pulley release
Diagnosis (as submitted): Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 107 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG), 2018 edition.

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 107 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Coverage requires a defined course of conservative therapy before A1 pulley release. Documentation does not show the required duration or type of conservative care.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with conservative treatment required. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination before or during the appeal process.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100055433) and reference UM-2026-104980. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30). She has been treated under commercial coverage with A1 pulley release recommended as the next appropriate step. Locking daily; failed steroid injection twice. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for A1 pulley release in the setting of Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
